title: Stamp Duty and Registration Charges in Tamil Nadu 2025
intro: The Government of Tamil Nadu levies stamp duty and registration charges on property transactions. In 2025, stamp duty is 7% and registration charges are up to 4% of the property value, making Tamil Nadu one of the states with the highest registration costs in India.

sections.content: Stamp duty and registration charges are mandatory costs paid during property registration. These charges vary from state to state and are governed by the Indian Stamp Act, 1899 and the Tamil Nadu Stamp Act, 2019.
What is Stamp Duty in Tamil Nadu?
Stamp duty is a legal tax paid to validate property documents. It applies to sale, resale, partition, lease, gift, and mortgage transactions and is compulsory for legal ownership.
Stamp Duty and Registration Charges in Tamil Nadu 2025
Stamp Duty and Registration Charges for Women

items: 1% discount on registration charges announced in March 2025
Applicable for properties up to Rs 10 lakh
Applies to houses, plots, and agricultural land

sections.content: Stamp Duty and Registration Charges for Different Deeds

rows: 1% on advance (or total if possession given)

sections.content: Example: Stamp Duty Calculation in Tamil Nadu
For a property worth Rs 30 lakh, total charges are 11% (7% stamp duty + 4% registration), amounting to Rs 3,30,000.
Registration of Settlement Deed in Tamil Nadu
Settlement deeds must be registered for validity. Stamp duty is 1% of market value (max Rs 25,000) and registration fee is 1% (max Rs 4,000).
Stamp Duty for Release Deed

items: Family members: 1% stamp duty (max Rs 25,000) and 1% registration (max Rs 4,000)
Non-family members: 7% stamp duty and 1% registration charge

sections.content: Stamp Duty for Lease Deed
Lease agreements attract 1% stamp duty and registration charges up to Rs 20,000. For leases above 99 years, stamp duty increases to 7%.
How to Calculate Stamp Duty in Tamil Nadu
Stamp duty is calculated on the guidance value. For a Rs 50 lakh property, stamp duty is Rs 3.5 lakh and registration charges are Rs 2 lakh.
How to Pay Stamp Duty in Tamil Nadu
Online Payment

items: Pay via NEFT, RTGS, DD, cash or cheque

sections.content: Offline Payment
Stamp duty can be paid at Sub-Registrar offices via cash, cheque, DD, NEFT, RTGS, debit or credit card.
Tax Benefits on Stamp Duty
Stamp duty and registration charges qualify for income tax deduction up to Rs 1.5 lakh under applicable sections.
Documents Required for Stamp Duty and Registration

items: Identity proof of buyer and witnesses

sections.content: Mandatory Mobile Number Rule (Dec 2025)
Property owner’s mobile number is now mandatory for registration to ensure transparency and prevent fraud.
Presenceless Property Registration in Tamil Nadu
GST on Land Registration in Tamil Nadu

items: Old property resale exempted from GST

sections.content: Stamp duty and registration charges are compulsory in Tamil Nadu. With stamp duty at 7% and registration charges up to 4%, buyers must plan finances carefully and always verify latest rates on the TNREGINET portal.
